title: rostellularia serpyllifolia
intro: Rostellularia serpyllifolia, also known as mint bush or mint-leaved mint bush, is a perennial plant species native to Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The plant has been used to treat digestive problems, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia, as well as respiratory issues like bronchitis and coughs. Rostellularia serpyllifolia is prepared in various herbal forms, including teas and tinctures, which can be used to take advantage of its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu.
